rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes
Place implementation annexes in valid implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when inserting annex blocks
Ensure annex blocks are placed in valid scopes according to AADL rules
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions to avoid unresolved references
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` for valid AGREE syntax
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Avoid placing assumptions or guarantees that refer to subcomponent ports in component type contracts
